results.title: Top shelf
results.brief_description: This stylish short animation borrows from the tradition of nastier fairytales. This creepy cautionary fable outlines what can happen to little girls who whine for toys.

results.title: A (Darren Almond)
results.brief_description:
Darren Almond’s film and video works create a tension between stillness and movement. The experience of place takes on a strong resonance in Almond’s minimalist but emotionally charged settings, which explore space, time and human subjectivity.

As an elemental ‘landscape simply made up of air, liquid and gas in different states’, Antarctica is a location that has long fascinated Almond. In early 2002, the artist was invited by Mission Antarctica to crew for a month around the Antarctic Peninsula. A is his response to that journey, in which Antarctica represents ‘the axis of time and space, the axis of everything’.

Almond’s haunting film envelopes the viewer in an abstract world, devoid of human presence. Skimming the crystalline surfaces of the ice islands, A slowly reveals delicate variations in the landscape as ice meets water and sky. The near-silence of Antarctica is matched with a dreamy score and the sampling of real sounds-cracks and rips in the ice-that Almond recorded on his journey. The visual and aural experience of A is as much an interior, psychological journey as an exterior one to the ‘end’ of the earth.

(SW 30.03.05)

results.title: The Light
results.brief_description:
Brian Doyle’s award-winning videos, installations and photos have been shown in galleries and film festivals throughout America and Europe. His latest body of work Yestermorrow (2000), Current (2001) and The Light
(2003) use the pretext of a developing storm to expose the ephemeral forces at play around us. Doyle documents phenomena that routinely affect our lives, uncovering alternate layers of reality below the surface of our recognition. Doyle’s collision of nature and culture invest everyday occurrences and familiar spaces with an otherworldly aura.

Doyle filmed across the United States in New York, Florida and Nevada, but The Light ultimately describes a non-place. Journeying from the countryside to urban construction grounds and the illumination of New York, light becomes a powerful metaphor for humanity’s struggle, through technology and determination,
to ‘pierce the night and see through the darkness’.

Beginning with the most simple of structures, a glowing street lamp, The Light gracefully charts a procession of increasingly complex illuminations. The film slowly builds toward the memorial light columns of the World Trade Center, which are shown not in direct conjunction with 9/11 but as the formal and abstracted transcendence of pure light.

(SW 30.03.05)

results.title: Memories of Moomba
results.brief_description: A selection of short films made in a Digital Storytelling workshop held to celebrate 50 years ofthe Moomba festival. The stories explore the history of the parade and life as a sun queen of moomba. It traces the history of the well known carnival family, and highlights the achievements of the key figures involved in the wild and wacky water events. Memories of Moomba was produced in collaboration with City of Melbourne and Melbourne Moomba Waterfest.

results.title: Quotes
results.brief_description:
A short created by a student for the 2004 ACMI Diegesis secondary school media festival.
This work features a group of rollerbladers displaying their moves, their falls and the tricks of their trade.

results.title: Going Solo
results.brief_description:
Through life experiences such as divorce, bereavement or never meeting the right soulmate we may find ourselves fifty plus and living on our own. For some in this situation a new partner is not what they crave, just the supportive friendship of those who understand.

Based on a series of interviews conducted with members of a Catholic Women’s solo group, this 36 min amateur documentary is a first time effort for the filmmaker and although the production values are at times questionable - the heart and soul of the women interviewed shine through making this a warm, often funny and inspirational account of life for a growing number of people in our community.

results.title: The good, the bad and the animated
results.brief_description: A short created by a student for the 2004 ACMI Diegesis secondary school media festival. This animated homage to Sergio Leone has the classic western ‘shootout’, between a cowboy in black and a cowboy in white. This town ain’t big enough for the both of them.
